entry.title: Washington Post: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Zelensky visits Mykolaiv; Severodonetsk hangs on
  • Dom Phillips, journalist who chronicled Amazon deforestation, is dead at 57
  • Bruno Pereira, expert on Brazil’s Indigenous communities, dead at 41
  • Britain will electronically tag some asylum seekers with GPS devices
  • How Cuba became a pioneer in covid-19 vaccines for kids
  • U.N. turns to crowdfunding to salvage oil tanker decaying off Yemen
  • In Slovyansk, conflicted loyalties as Russian forces approach
  • European Commission backs Kyiv’s E.U. ambitions as Putin lashes out at West
  • With scant options in Ukraine, U.S. and allies prepare for long war
  • Opposition rally demands early elections in North Macedonia
  • Brazil: police arrest 3rd suspect in Amazon shooting deaths
  • Scholz: G7 will support Ukraine 'for as long as necessary'
  • Russia frees captive medic who filmed Mariupol's horror
  • Brazil Indigenous expert was 'bigger target' in recent years
  • Celia, Blas weaken, but still bring heavy rain, surf
  • Floods in India, Bangladesh leave millions homeless, 18 dead
  • Germany's Green party says email system hit by cyberattack
  • Officials: Militants target labor camp in Pakistan, kill 3
  • At least 1 killed in attack on Sikh temple in Afghan capital
  • Lawmakers urge Google to fix abortion searches suggesting ‘fake clinics’
  • Her dog died suddenly. Then a Chewy delivery brought a surprise.
  • Local and state Democrats look to ban gun sales near schools
  • Montana Gov. Gianforte vacationed in Italy as flooding crushed Yellowstone
  • Biden takes a spill on a morning bike ride at the beach
  • Post Politics Now: Trump attacks Jan. 6 committee, Pence and Barr in speech to conservative group
  • Bitcoin price falls below $20,000 for first time since 2020
  • U.S. policymakers misjudged inflation threat until it was too late
  • How to recession-proof your life
  • Americans are starting to pull back on travel and restaurants
  • Why Is It So Hard to Find a Decent Public Bathroom?
  • Why Is the Cost of Rent Going Up? Ask the Fed
  • Why Supreme Court Watchers Are Making Bingo Jokes
  • The Prince of Wales and His Discontents
  • Gov. Ron DeSantis abandons Florida children
  • Juneteenth reminds us of an often forgotten side of the liberation struggle
  • Daniel Snyder proves why taxpayers should not help build his stadium
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Avid air mile fans fly to other end of Japan for quick break, head home
  • Push to make hospitals accept infected patients faces opposition
  • Mother handed 5-year prison term for starving son, 5, to death
  • Old enough? Kids on first errands rivet viewers, stir parenting debate
  • Kishida visits plants where labor is backer of the opposition
  • Japan protests to China over resource development in East China Sea
  • Head of U.S. Marines Pacific to bolster defense in south Japan
  • Rating system to start in Osaka for private ‘minpaku’ tourist facilities
  • Kuroda admits weak yen hurts but keeps loose monetary policy
  • Riken tests solve 132-year mystery of ‘missing link’ creature
  • Olympic filmmaker needed 2 movies to cover Tokyo turmoil
  • EDITORIAL: Top court ignores state’s duty to do utmost to secure nuclear safety
  • VOX POPULI: ‘Gerrymanders’ not visible on Japan’s redrawn electoral map
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 18 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 18 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-18 22:45PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Masks, Covid-19 test kits, plastic bottles: more than 9,000kg of litter found on Hong Kong’s hiking trails, seashore last year, green group says
  • Fraudsters swindled HK$1.28 billion from more than 6,000 Hongkongers through online, phone scams in first 4 months of year
  • Hong Kong’s buyers snap up 70 per cent of new homes on offer, casting aside HKMA’s 75-point interest hike as banks deferred raising mortgage rates

  • Greater Bay Area: can China ‘integrate’ Hong Kong into its southern economic powerhouse?

  • Chinese President Xi Jinping warns against international decoupling in Russia speech
  • Biden urges China, other major economies to step up climate action, as US and EU join global methane cuts pledge
  • China to tighten grip on social media comments, requiring sites to employ sufficient content moderators
  • China’s latest hot export is a US$50 toy Russian fighter jet that ‘will never break’, helped by social media such as TikTok
  • Huge blaze at Shanghai Sinopec plant kills one
  • Chinese university student wins Apple’s Swift coding challenge with genetics experiment simulation
  • Coronavirus: which works better to handle a pandemic – democracy or autocracy?
  • Tencent’s WeChat debuts in 618 shopping festival with big bet on live-streaming e-commerce despite slowing economy
  • China’s US$1.5 trillion opportunity: private pension scheme entices Fidelity, Manulife, Invesco, Hong Kong asset managers
  • China manufacturing: fresh lay-offs in the Pearl River Delta add to worries over economic slowdown
  • Singapore’s Pink Dot gay pride rally returns, as MP from ruling party attends for first time – and in a pink T-shirt
  • ‘Never seen such floods in her life’: 41 dead, millions stranded in India, Bangladesh
  • The way we wed: how Indian brides are reinventing ceremonies to push for gender equality
  • Oil-producing Gulf nations are pursuing greener energy. What does this mean for Asia?
  • US, Marshall Islands to speed up economic assistance deal to counter China’s Pacific influence
  • As Japan court ruling nears, LGBT groups hope for new milestone in fight for equality
  • China’s Covid-19 health code system is ripe for abuse and must not outlast the pandemic
  • Why Singapore may be edging closer to scrapping Section 377A criminalising gay sex – and what would happen if it did
  • Warplanes hit Hamas sites in Gaza in retaliation to rocket fire, Israeli army says
  • Spain battles to bring fires under control as it swelters in heatwave
  • Defrocked Chilean priest sentenced to 15 years in prison for sexually abusing minors
  • Ukraine plans to resume peace talks with Russia by August, officials say
  • Coronavirus: vaccine roll-out for American children under 5 close after CDC panel vote
  • ‘Bloodsicles’ and ice baths: How US zookeepers coax animals through heatwaves
  • Brazil police arrest third suspect in Amazon shooting deaths
  • Kate Bush classic Running Up That Hill tops UK chart after 37 years
  • Gunman, 70, kills 3 senior citizens during potluck dinner at Alabama church
  • US billionaire Warren Buffett’s last charity private lunch sees record US$19 million bid
  • Russia frees Ukraine medic who filmed Mariupol horror, saved both sides’ people
  • Boris sings out for Ukraine: UK PM Johnson says nation deserves to host Eurovision
  • UK begins electronically tagging migrants coming via ‘unnecessary, dangerous routes’
  • Woman arrested for making anti-Asian remarks, pepper spraying 4 people in New York
  • Ukraine war: captured US fighters Alexander Drueke and Andy Huynh surface in Russian state TV videos
  • Biden says meeting with crown prince not focus of Saudi Arabia trip amid criticism
  • SpaceX fires workers behind letter criticising Elon Musk’s behaviour
  • Google co-founder Sergey Brin seeks divorce from second wife Nicole Shanahan after 3 years of marriage
  • Bitcoin’s cascading losses accelerate in record-breaking rout
  • Post-lockdown Shanghai among top spending cities during China’s 618 online shopping extravaganza
  • ByteDance closes Shanghai games studio in retreat from industry dominated by Tencent, NetEase
  • Maggie Cheung in her own words, on Wong Kar-wai and In the Mood for Love, marrying Olivier Assayas and being an actress, not just a film star
  • Sweet Home on Netflix: seasons 2 and 3, new stars for monster K-drama series
  • ‘He shared with me his experience with stress – that was really helpful’: why men should face mental health issues head on and talk about them
  • Why sommeliers hate it when you BYOB – a Hong Kong insider explains why
  • Taylor Swift then and now: 8 things you might not know about the singer, from her early financial adviser dreams to why she feels like an outsider
  • Traditional artisans front and centre of tourism promotion in Hanoi as Vietnamese capital seeks to preserve dying craftsman communities
  • Zero-waste bulk food and eco-friendly product retailer Live Zero in Hong Kong to open new flagship store, part of founder’s fresh strategy
  • The Chinatown Stalin made a ghost town: Millionka in Vladivostok, home for labourers who built the Russian Far East
  • K-drama midseason recap: Kiss Sixth Sense – Disney+’s muddled fantasy romance is seriously in need of an 11th-hour rescue
  • Who are the richest Fast & Furious stars? Net worths, ranked – from Dwayne ‘The Rock’ Johnson and Vin Diesel, to Wonder Woman’s Gal Gadot and the late Paul Walker, here’s who raked in millions
  • Meet Elon Musk’s sister Tosca, founder of Netflix-style streaming service Passionflix, accomplished filmmaker and mother of twins – but did her brother invest in her company?
  • 13 little-known facts about Lightyear star Chris Evans, from crushing on Sandra Bullock and matching Avengers tattoos with Scarlett Johansson, to starring in a Marilyn Manson music video
  • The luxury life of Formula One’s George Russell: how the Mercedes driver and star of Netflix’s Formula 1: Drive to Survive spends big on luxury cars, designer suits and holidays with his girlfriend
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • A Defiant Putin Says Russia Will Flourish Without the West
  • Russians Breached This City, Not With Troops, but Propaganda
  • Amid Jan. 6 Revelations, Election Lies Still Dominate the G.O.P.
  • The Watergate Hearings, 50 Years Ago: Truth Was Not Up for Debate
  • Uterine Cancer Is on the Rise, Especially Among Black Women
  • The Coin That Could Wreck Crypto
  • ‘He Took Jan. 6 Personally’
  • Colombia Will Soon Have Its First Black Female Vice President. Will It Be Her?
  • In These New Textbooks, Hong Kong Was Never a British Colony
  • An ugly week for stocks leaves the S&P 500 with its biggest loss since March 2020.
  • Infighting Overshadows Big Plans at The Washington Post
  • A Decade After DACA, the Rise of a New Generation of Undocumented Students
  • A Gull Flaps Its Wings and a Deadly Virus Explodes
  • Stephen Curry Left His Critics With Nothing Else to Say
  • Rafael Nadal, Chasing Grand Slam, Will Head to Wimbledon
  • ‘The Old Man’ Brought Jeff Bridges to TV. John Lithgow Had No Advice.
  • New York Philharmonic Chooses Arts Veteran as Leader
  • The Rush to E.S.G., With or Without Elon Musk
  • Covid Vaccine and Fisheries Deals Close a ‘Roller Coaster’ W.T.O. Meeting
  • BTS Ponders Its Future, and South Korea’s Economy Warily Takes Note
  • SpaceX Said to Fire Employees Involved in Letter Rebuking Elon Musk
  • With Funding Stalled, Chip Makers Warn Congress the U.S. Is Lagging
  • Why Some States Are Expanding Tax-Free Periods
  • My Father Makes Me So Angry. I Take Care of Him Anyway.
  • Crypto, Houses, Sneakers, Rolexes: How FOMO Drove the Economy
  • Pregnancy Can Affect the Body Forever. Have Abortion Foes Reckoned With That?
  • Something Extraordinary Is Happening in France
  • We Should Thank Mike Pence for His Courage on Jan. 6
  • A Filmmaker Imagines a Japan Where the Elderly Volunteer to Die
  • China Launches Third Aircraft Carrier in Major Milestone for Xi Jinping
  • A High-Flying Italian Surgeon’s Fall From Grace
  • On the edge of embattled twin cities, an uncovered mass grave for civilians grows.
  • Torched Trains and Burning Tires: India’s New Military Recruitment Plan Fuels Angry Protests
  • ‘Wholly Unexpected’: These Polar Bears Can Survive With Less Sea Ice
  • She Spoke Out Against Vietnam’s Plans for Coal. Then She Was Arrested.
  • Fuel-Starved and Stuck in Crisis, Sri Lanka Orders Work from Home
  • Man Paralyzed 12 Years Ago Becomes Italy’s First Assisted Suicide
  • Greek Court Rejects Effort to Toughen Punishment After Killing of an American
  • Jan. 6 Panel Could Start Sharing Transcripts With Justice Dept. as Soon as July
  • A Day After a Portrait of Pence in Danger, Trump Attacks Him Again
  • A man who threatened Colorado’s secretary of state on Instagram faces two years in prison.
  • A Better Way to Measure Immunity in Children
  • F.D.A. Authorizes Moderna and Pfizer Covid Vaccines for Youngest Children
  • N.Y.C.’s vaccine plan for children younger than 5 relies on pediatricians and vaccine hubs.
  • Fast-Tracked Bill Stokes Fears of Private Development in 1,200-Acre Park
  • The Jury in the Bill Cosby Sex Assault Case Says It’s Close to a Verdict
  • New York City Estimates More Unsheltered People, but Comparisons Are Tricky
  • Uvalde Officer Passed Up Shot at Gunman for Fear of Hitting Children
  • Police Error Led to Trump-Era Subpoena to Twitter About a Devin Nunes Critic
  • N.J. Transit Shuts Down Train Service, Blaming Engineer Sickout
  • Jean-Louis Trintignant, Star of Celebrated European Films, Dies at 91
  • Tim Sale, Comic Book Artist Who Reimagined Batman, Dies at 66
  • Quotation of the Day: Kremlin Propaganda Softens Up Targets
  • Corrections: June 18, 2022
  • Jayson Tatum Knew He Could Have Had His Moment
  • A Weird, Wild and Entirely Typical Day at the U.S. Open
  • An Exclusive Reward at the U.S. Open: The Club’s Signature Cocktail
  • Why Is the World’s Best Swimmer Not Swimming at the Worlds?
  • She Won a Tony. But Deirdre O’Connell ‘Can’t Think About That.’
  • Review: The Met Opera Orchestra Raises a Glorious Noise
  • Summer Horror in the City
  • Review: In ‘Queen,’ the Numbers Don’t Always Add Up
  • Brontez Purnell Brings His Disparate Parts Back to the Dance Stage
  • Drake Releases ‘Honestly, Nevermind,’ an LP Dedicated to Virgil Abloh
Today's New York Times
